name: Agent Lifecycle
summary: Manages the full lifecycle of AI agent sessions including creation, destruction, abort, and persistence. Agent sessions are stored in SQLite via Prisma with configuration, status, and message history.
goals: Create agent sessions with configurable model, permission mode, and system prompt
Destroy and abort running agent sessions cleanly
Persist session state and message history to SQLite for session restoration
Restore agent sessions on server restart

acceptanceCriteria.given: A project path and agent type are provided
acceptanceCriteria.then: a session is created in the database with status 'running' and a unique ID
the session is removed from memory and marked as destroyed in the database
the agent process is terminated and status is set to 'idle'

acceptanceCriteria.given: Server restarts with existing agent sessions

unit-test.when: the adapter fails to create the session
unit-test.then: an error is returned and no session is persisted

end-to-end.when: user creates an agent, sends a message, then destroys it
end-to-end.then: agent lifecycle completes without errors and messages are persisted

syncMeta.baseCommitMessage: feat(sync-specs): add auto-detection status rules in Step 5 user review
